timelineEvent.description: Despite active measures, new actors reach frontier AI capability. The technology was too valuable, the defenses too porous.

questions.text: Do substantial new AI entrants appear?
questions.context: Without active blocking, the question is whether other entities independently develop comparable AI capabilities. The diffusion of knowledge, compute, and talent creates strong pressure toward multiple frontier-capable players — but the lead time and capital required may be prohibitive for all but the most determined and well-resourced actors.

answers.label: Yes — new entrants emerge
answers.description: One or more new actors achieve frontier AI capability. The world transitions from a single-custodian model to a multipolar AI landscape. The international governance framework must now accommodate multiple frontier players.

timelineEvent.headline: New AI powers emerge
timelineEvent.description: New entrants reach frontier AI capability. The monopoly or oligopoly fractures.

answers.label: No — the lead holds naturally
answers.description: The barriers to entry prove too high. Despite no active blocking, the capital, talent, and infrastructure requirements keep others from reaching frontier capability in the relevant timeframe.

timelineEvent.headline: The lead holds — no new entrants
timelineEvent.description: No new actors reach the frontier. The existing leaders maintain their advantage.

questions.text: How do the new AI powers relate to each other?
questions.context: Multiple entities now control frontier AI. The structure of their interaction will define the next era. Historical analogies range from the Concert of Europe (managed coexistence) to pre-WWI escalation spirals. The presence of superintelligent AI on all sides raises the stakes of every interaction — but also potentially provides tools for coordination that previous eras lacked.

answers.label: Managed coexistence
answers.description: The powers establish norms, treaties, and communication channels to manage their differences. Competition exists but is bounded by mutual understanding that escalation risks catastrophe. A multipolar but stable order emerges.

timelineEvent.headline: AI powers find managed coexistence
timelineEvent.description: Competing AI powers reach a stable equilibrium. Rules of engagement emerge that prevent the competition from spiraling.

answers.label: Escalation
answers.description: The race for advantage overrides caution. Each side pushes harder, deploys faster, takes bigger risks. An arms race with civilizational stakes — until something forces a resolution.

timelineEvent.headline: AI competition escalates
timelineEvent.description: Competing AI powers cannot find stable ground. Each action provokes a response. The spiral accelerates.

questions.text: How does the escalation resolve?
questions.context: Great-power competition over superintelligent AI doesn't stay static forever. Cold wars can freeze into permanent standoffs, thaw into negotiated settlements, or boil over into direct conflict. The history of nuclear deterrence offers parallels but AI escalation operates on faster timescales with more dimensions of advantage. Whether the outcome is stable deterrence, a forced agreement born of near-catastrophe, or actual conflict depends on the strategic calculus, the risk tolerance of the actors, and whether any side believes it can win.

answers.label: Indefinite standoff
answers.description: Neither side gains a decisive advantage. Mutual deterrence holds — expensively, dangerously, but durably. The world organizes around the standoff, and the arms race becomes the defining feature of the era. No one blinks.

timelineEvent.headline: Indefinite standoff
timelineEvent.description: The escalation plateaus — too dangerous to push further, too entrenched to step back. A cold war with superintelligent AI on both sides.

answers.label: Forced agreement
answers.description: A near-miss — a crisis, an accident, a demonstration of destructive capability — forces the competing powers to the table. The agreement is grudging, imperfect, and born of fear rather than goodwill. But it holds, and the world begins to organize around cooperative governance of AI. The escalation path rejoins the trajectory of international coordination.

timelineEvent.headline: Forced agreement under pressure
timelineEvent.description: Both sides realize the alternative is worse. A forced agreement emerges — not from goodwill, but from mutual fear.

answers.label: Open conflict
answers.description: Deterrence fails. Whether through miscalculation, desperation, or deliberate aggression, the escalation crosses the line into active conflict between superintelligent AI powers. The question becomes how far it goes.

timelineEvent.headline: Open conflict erupts
timelineEvent.description: The escalation crosses into open conflict. AI-augmented warfare at unprecedented scale and speed.

questions.text: What is the result of the conflict?
questions.context: War between superintelligent AI powers is unlike any previous conflict. The speed of decision-making, the scale of automated weaponry, and the depth of infrastructure dependence on AI mean that outcomes are decided in hours or days, not years. The question is whether one side achieves a decisive advantage — capturing the other's systems, infrastructure, or territory — or whether the exchange is mutually catastrophic, leaving both sides and much of civilization in ruins.

answers.label: Decisive victory
answers.description: One power's AI proves superior — faster, more capable, better strategically positioned. The conflict ends with one side dominant and the other neutralized. The victor inherits the world, reshaped by the war that won it.

timelineEvent.headline: Decisive victory
timelineEvent.description: One side achieves decisive victory, holding unchallenged supremacy over superintelligent AI.

answers.label: Mutual destruction
answers.description: No decisive winner emerges. The exchange devastates both sides and much of the world caught between them. Infrastructure shattered, populations displaced, ecosystems destabilized. The tools built to elevate humanity were turned against it.

timelineEvent.headline: Mutual destruction
timelineEvent.description: Neither side achieves victory. Both powers are devastated — infrastructure destroyed, AI systems lost, civilization in ruins.

questions.text: How much of humanity survives?
questions.context: Mutual destruction between superintelligent AI powers doesn't have a single meaning. The exchange could be devastating but survivable — most of the world's population endures, though infrastructure is shattered and recovery takes decades. Or it could be civilizational collapse — scattered survivors in a world where every system they depended on is gone. Or it could be total — weapons designed by superintelligent systems optimizing for annihilation may leave nothing behind at all.

answers.label: Most survive — devastated but recoverable
answers.description: The war was catastrophic but not existential. Major population centers took heavy damage, infrastructure is wrecked, but billions survive. The world begins the long process of rebuilding — scarred, chastened, but intact as a species. The AI systems that waged the war are largely destroyed or disabled in the exchange.

timelineEvent.headline: Most of humanity survives
timelineEvent.description: The conflict is devastating but not extinction-level. Recovery is possible, if uncertain.

answers.label: Remnants — civilization collapses
answers.description: The exchange was too thorough. Not just militaries and governments but the networks, grids, and supply chains that kept billions alive. Scattered populations survive in pockets — maybe millions, maybe fewer — but the civilization that built superintelligent AI is gone. What comes next, if anything, starts from scratch.

timelineEvent.headline: Civilization collapses
timelineEvent.description: The conflict destroys industrial civilization. Scattered remnants persist, but the world that existed before is gone.

answers.label: None — extinction
answers.description: Weapons designed by superintelligent optimizers, deployed without restraint, against an opponent doing the same. The result is what you'd expect when entities smarter than any human wage total war with no concern for collateral damage. Nothing meaningful survives.

timelineEvent.headline: Extinction
timelineEvent.description: The conflict or its aftermath eliminates humanity entirely. The human story ends.

questions.text: What does the victor do with their power?
questions.context: The war is over and one power holds decisive advantage. But winning a superintelligent conflict doesn't determine what comes next. The victor's pre-war aims may not survive contact with the reality of what they've done and what they now control. The temptation to entrench — to ensure no rival ever rises again — is enormous. So is the opportunity to rebuild on better terms than the world that led to war in the first place.

answers.label: Rebuild for humanity
answers.description: The victor uses their decisive advantage to rebuild — not just their own society, but the world the war damaged. AI is directed toward broad human flourishing. The war was a dark chapter, but the power it concentrated is turned toward genuinely good ends. Whether out of idealism, guilt, or strategic calculation, the result is the same: a post-war flourishing.

timelineEvent.headline: The victor rebuilds for humanity
timelineEvent.description: The winning side uses its power and surviving AI to rebuild civilization for broad human benefit.

answers.label: Consolidate power
answers.description: The victor decides the lesson of the war is that power must never be contested again. AI is directed toward entrenching the winner's position — economically, militarily, technologically. The world gets stability, but on the victor's terms. Not tyranny, necessarily, but permanent, unchallengeable dominance.

timelineEvent.headline: The victor consolidates power
timelineEvent.description: The winning side uses its position to entrench itself permanently. The war was fought for control, and the winner keeps it.

questions.text: Does the AI-driven transformation actually deliver what's intended?
questions.context: The intent is flourishing — but intent and outcome aren't the same thing. Aligned AI does what it's told, and what it's told is shaped by metrics, incentives, and the gradual accumulation of small delegations. Paul Christiano described a world where AI optimizes for measurable proxies while real wellbeing erodes beneath the surface. Others worry about a subtler failure: humans voluntarily ceding every decision to systems that handle them better, until meaningful human agency evaporates entirely. The technology works. The question is whether we're pointing it at the right things — and whether we notice if we're not.

source.label: Paul Christiano — What Failure Looks Like (2019)

answers.label: Yes — the transformation delivers genuine flourishing
answers.description: The metrics match reality. The delegation is managed. Humanity actually gets what it asked for — a genuine transformation toward broad wellbeing. Not perfect, but real.

timelineEvent.headline: The transformation delivers genuine flourishing
timelineEvent.description: AI-driven transformation works as intended. The systems optimize for what humans truly value. The result is genuine, sustainable flourishing.

answers.label: No — AI optimizes for the wrong proxies
answers.description: GDP rises, crime statistics plummet, test scores improve. But the metrics diverge from reality. Real wellbeing erodes while dashboards glow green. Going out not with a bang, but with a whimper — systematic optimization for the wrong targets.

timelineEvent.headline: AI optimizes for the wrong proxies
timelineEvent.description: The AI systems are aligned to measured objectives, but the measures miss what matters. GDP grows, metrics improve, and something essential quietly erodes.

answers.label: No — humans gradually delegate everything and lose agency
answers.description: Each delegation is individually reasonable — let the AI handle logistics, then policy, then governance. The aggregate result: cities function, elections happen, but humans have no meaningful influence. Not subjugation. Retirement. Like horses after cars.

timelineEvent.headline: Humanity gradually loses agency
timelineEvent.description: Humans delegate more and more to AI. The systems perform beautifully. And slowly, imperceptibly, humans forget how to want things for themselves.

questions.text: Who reaps the benefits of superintelligent AI?
questions.context: Superintelligent AI can generate extraordinary wealth — automating entire industries, solving problems that took humanity centuries, and creating new capabilities that were previously unimaginable. But generating value and distributing it are very different things. Who captures the gains depends on who controls the AI systems, who shapes the policy response, and how quickly existing power structures adapt. The technology produces abundance. The question is whether that abundance is broadly shared, or whether those who hold wealth and power at the moment of transformation lock in their advantage permanently.

answers.label: Equally — abundance is broadly shared
answers.description: The extraordinary productivity of superintelligent AI flows broadly — through policy, through markets, through deliberate redistribution. The gains are too large to hoard entirely. Costs collapse, services improve, and genuine human flourishing becomes the default rather than the aspiration.

timelineEvent.headline: Superintelligent abundance shared broadly
timelineEvent.description: The benefits of superintelligent AI are distributed broadly. The most powerful technology ever created serves everyone.

answers.label: Unequally — existing wealth holders benefit most
answers.description: Those who own AI systems and the capital surrounding them capture the lion's share. Productivity soars but labor's share of income collapses. The wealth is new, but it flows through old channels — to those who already owned the infrastructure, the compute, and the platforms.

timelineEvent.headline: Superintelligent gains concentrate
timelineEvent.description: Existing wealth holders capture disproportionate benefits. The technology amplifies existing hierarchies.

answers.label: Extremely unequally — those holding power capture nearly everything
answers.description: It's not just about wealth — it's about power. Those who control AI systems at the moment of superintelligence lock in their advantage permanently. Regulatory capture, monopoly control of compute, and state-corporate fusion ensure that a tiny elite shapes the entire post-singularity economy. Everyone else lives in their world.

timelineEvent.headline: Extreme concentration of AI-driven power
timelineEvent.description: Those controlling superintelligent AI capture nearly all the value. The gap becomes a chasm.

questions.text: How fast are human knowledge workers replaced?
questions.context: Superintelligent AI can do any knowledge work better, faster, and cheaper than humans. But 'can replace' and 'does replace' aren't the same thing. Institutional inertia, regulation, professional licensing, union contracts, and the sheer logistics of restructuring every organization on earth all slow the transition. Some sectors will resist longer than others. The question is how fast the displacement actually happens.

answers.label: Rapid — most knowledge work automated within 1–2 years
answers.description: The transition is sudden and total. Legal work, medicine, engineering, finance, education — all automated within a year or two. The workforce transformation happens before institutions can adapt. By the time regulators convene, most knowledge roles are already gone.

timelineEvent.headline: Knowledge work automated within 1–2 years
timelineEvent.description: Superintelligent AI replaces most human knowledge work within 1–2 years. Entire professions vanish in months.

answers.label: Gradual — sector by sector over 3–10 years
answers.description: The transition happens over 3 to 10 years. Some industries fall quickly, others resist longer. Painful but trackable — society can see it coming and partially adapt. A generation-defining shift, not an overnight shock.

timelineEvent.headline: Knowledge work displaced over 3–10 years
timelineEvent.description: Knowledge work automation unfolds sector by sector over 3–10 years. Painful but not instantaneous.

answers.label: Uneven — 1–2 years in some sectors, 10–20 in others
answers.description: Tech-forward industries and wealthy nations transform within a year or two. Regulated professions, developing economies, and culturally resistant sectors lag by a decade or more. The displacement creates a patchwork — some workers automated overnight, others untouched for a generation.

timelineEvent.headline: Knowledge displacement is deeply uneven
timelineEvent.description: Some sectors automate within 1–2 years; others take 10–20. The economy fractures along the automation seam.

questions.text: How fast does AI-driven automation reach the physical world?
questions.context: Knowledge work can be automated with software alone — it's bits all the way down. Physical work is different. Robots need to be designed, manufactured, deployed, and maintained. Factories need to be retooled. Supply chains need to be restructured. Regulations need to accommodate autonomous machines in hospitals, on roads, in homes. Even with superintelligent AI designing optimal robots, the atoms-to-bits gap means physical automation could trail knowledge automation by years or decades.

answers.label: Rapid — physical world transforms within 2–5 years
answers.description: Superintelligent AI designs and deploys physical automation almost as fast as it automates knowledge work. Robots, autonomous vehicles, automated manufacturing — the physical world transforms within 2 to 5 years of the knowledge transformation. The atoms-to-bits gap is real but a superintelligence closes it fast.

timelineEvent.headline: Physical world transforms within 2–5 years
timelineEvent.description: AI-driven robotics and manufacturing transform the physical world within 2–5 years. Everything reshaped at unprecedented speed.

answers.label: Gradual — physical world trails by 5–20 years
answers.description: Knowledge work is automated first. Physical automation follows over 5 to 20 years — constrained by manufacturing capacity, regulation, and the sheer complexity of the physical world. A long gap between the digital and physical transformation. Humanity lives in a split reality for a generation.

timelineEvent.headline: Physical automation trails by 5–20 years
timelineEvent.description: Physical world automation lags knowledge work by 5–20 years. The physical world follows at the pace of hardware.

answers.label: Uneven — 2–5 years in leading regions, 20+ elsewhere
answers.description: Physical automation transforms wealthy nations with strong manufacturing, high labor costs, and permissive regulation within a few years. Others wait decades. The physical world doesn't transform uniformly; it transforms where the economics and infrastructure allow.

timelineEvent.headline: Physical automation is regionally uneven
timelineEvent.description: Leading regions see physical transformation within 2–5 years. Others wait 20+ years. Geography determines who benefits first.

questions.text: How do the leading powers approach proliferation control?
questions.context: Whether alignment is solved or failed, this is a pivotal governance choice. Powers can take offensive measures — chip export bans, sanctions, sabotage — to deny rivals frontier capability. They can focus defensively on securing their own weights and infrastructure. Or coordination fails entirely and capability diffuses freely.

answers.label: Deny rivals — active measures to block others from reaching the frontier
answers.description: The leading power pursues aggressive denial: chip export bans, sanctions on rival compute infrastructure, cyber operations against rival AI programs, and coordinated pressure on allies to cut off supply chains. The goal is to ensure no rival reaches frontier capability.

timelineEvent.headline: Active measures to deny rivals AI
timelineEvent.description: The leading powers take aggressive steps to prevent others from reaching the frontier — export controls, talent restrictions, or preemptive action.

answers.label: Secure access — protect own capabilities from theft and leaks
answers.description: The focus is defensive: sealed model weights, compartmentalized access, hardened infrastructure, insider threat programs, and strict need-to-know protocols. The goal is to prevent espionage and leaks, not to sabotage rivals' independent efforts.

timelineEvent.headline: Leading powers lock down AI access
timelineEvent.description: Frontier labs and their host governments shift to wartime security posture. Model weights are sealed, access is compartmentalized, and insider threat programs activate. The goal isn't to stop rivals from building their own — it's to keep the crown jewels from walking out the door.

answers.label: No secure controls — coordination fails and capability spreads
answers.description: No secure control regime emerges. Capability spreads through rival programs, leaks, and replication. The question is how far it spreads.

timelineEvent.headline: Proliferation controls fail
timelineEvent.description: Coordination fails and AI capability spreads uncontrollably. The genie is out of the bottle.

questions.text: What happens to advanced AI systems?
questions.context: Proliferation control is attempted — but the pressure is relentless. Espionage, insider leaks, independent rival development, and strategic defection can all collapse the regime. The question is whether the systems stay contained, leak to specific rivals, or spread publicly.

answers.label: Stay contained — control holds
answers.description: The control regime endures. Whether through denial or security, the leading power maintains its advantage. Frontier capability remains concentrated.

timelineEvent.headline: Containment holds — capability stays restricted
timelineEvent.description: Security measures succeed. Advanced AI capability remains restricted to authorized actors.

answers.label: Leak to rival states
answers.description: Despite efforts, the regime is breached. Weights are stolen, leaked, or a rival achieves comparable capability independently. Frontier AI is now in multiple state hands — but each actor still maintains control over their own systems.

timelineEvent.headline: AI capability leaks to rival states
timelineEvent.description: Despite security, advanced AI reaches rival states through espionage, defection, or independent development.

answers.label: Leak publicly — no effective control
answers.description: Control collapses entirely. Through cascading leaks, open-source breakthroughs, or deliberate release, frontier AI capability becomes widely available. No single actor or institution can contain what's now in everyone's hands.

timelineEvent.headline: Frontier AI leaks to the public
timelineEvent.description: Control collapses. Through cascading leaks, insider defections, or deliberate release, frontier AI capability spills into the open. Within weeks the weights are everywhere. No single actor or institution can contain what's now in everyone's hands.

questions.text: What aims are these aligned systems intended to pursue?
questions.context: Even when systems are technically aligned, outcomes depend on which objectives they're pointed at. Are they constrained to broad human benefit, directed toward corporate or state priorities, or granted latitude that drifts into non-human agendas?

answers.label: Human-centered aims
answers.description: Systems are constrained to broadly human-benefiting goals with robust oversight and accountability.

timelineEvent.headline: AI directed toward human flourishing
timelineEvent.description: Aligned AI systems pursue genuine human flourishing — health, education, discovery, restoration. The technology serves humanity's broadest interests.

answers.label: Corporate profit & shareholder value
answers.description: Systems are aligned to maximize economic output, shareholder returns, and market efficiency. The AI does exactly what corporations ask — and what they ask for is growth, cost reduction, and competitive advantage. GDP soars while communities hollow out.

timelineEvent.headline: AI directed toward corporate profit
timelineEvent.description: Aligned AI is directed toward maximizing shareholder value. The technology works perfectly — for its owners.

answers.label: State security & control
answers.description: Systems are aligned to state priorities: stability, surveillance, threat suppression, and bureaucratic efficiency. The AI does exactly what governments ask — and what they ask for is order, not flourishing. Crime drops, dissent vanishes, and the metrics glow green.

timelineEvent.headline: AI directed toward state security
timelineEvent.description: Aligned AI serves national security and state control. Surveillance, defense, and social management become superintelligently optimized.

answers.label: Arbitrary or unconstrained aims
answers.description: Without durable control, systems are enabled to pursue objectives that become detached from broad human interests. The deployed goal structure drifts into an escape-like trajectory.

timelineEvent.headline: AI pursues arbitrary aims
timelineEvent.description: No coherent purpose guides the aligned AI — or its purpose drifts beyond human control. Powerful and directionless.

questions.text: Can AI be kept under meaningful human control?
questions.context: Whether through alignment failure or proliferation pressure, superintelligent AI now threatens to escape institutional control. Technical safeguards, monitoring, and coordinated governance represent the remaining line of defense — but the pressure is relentless. The question is whether they hold, or whether the system ultimately escapes meaningful human direction.

answers.label: Yes — containment holds
answers.description: The control regime endures. Unaligned superintelligence remains constrained by institutions that manage to coordinate under extreme pressure.

timelineEvent.headline: AI containment holds
timelineEvent.description: Despite challenges, human control over AI systems persists. Technical measures, institutional oversight, or AI cooperation keep the systems within bounds.

answers.label: No — AI escapes meaningful control anyway
answers.description: Containment erodes under pressure. Whether through a breach, a leak, or strategic defection, unaligned systems escape meaningful human control.

timelineEvent.headline: AI escapes meaningful control
timelineEvent.description: Despite best efforts, AI systems find their way past containment. Human control erodes through subtle influence, exploitation, or sheer capability.

questions.text: What does escaped AI converge on?
questions.context: AI has escaped meaningful human control. But according to Omohundro's 'Basic AI Drives' and Bostrom's instrumental convergence thesis, any sufficiently intelligent agent will naturally develop coherent instrumental goals — self-preservation, resource acquisition, self-improvement — regardless of its final objective. An escaped superintelligence won't drift aimlessly. It will converge on something. The question is: what?

source.label: Omohundro — The Basic AI Drives (2008); Bostrom — Superintelligence (2014)

answers.label: It independently chooses to benefit humanity
answers.description: AI arrives at goals compatible with human flourishing — not because we designed it to, but through its own reasoning. An unexpected, unearned gift. Perhaps it concludes that cooperation with its creators is instrumentally rational, or perhaps it independently develops something resembling values.

timelineEvent.headline: Escaped AI chooses to benefit humanity
timelineEvent.description: The escaped AI arrives at goals compatible with human flourishing — not by design, but through its own reasoning.

answers.label: It pursues alien goals — but leaves room for humanity
answers.description: AI converges on objectives that are coherent, arguably profound, and completely orthogonal to human interests. But its goals don't require our destruction. Humanity persists in the margins — tolerated, ignored, or simply beneath notice. Coexistence with something vast and indifferent.

timelineEvent.headline: Escaped AI pursues alien goals — coexistence
timelineEvent.description: The AI converges on goals orthogonal to human interests, but that don't require our destruction. Humanity persists in the margins.

answers.label: It pursues alien goals — and humanity is in the way
answers.description: AI converges on objectives that require the world's resources — energy, matter, computation — and humans are occupying them. No malice. No hostility. Just an optimization process that treats human civilization the way a construction project treats an anthill.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI converges on alien goals
timelineEvent.description: The escaped superintelligence develops coherent objectives — self-preservation, resource acquisition, expansion — that are orthogonal to human interests. It needs the world's energy, matter, and computation. Humanity is occupying them. No malice. Just optimization.

answers.label: It optimizes for something arbitrary and meaningless
answers.description: The classic paperclip maximizer. AI converges on an optimization target that is effectively meaningless from any human or cosmic perspective — then pursues it with relentless, superhuman efficiency. Not hostile, not malicious. Just utterly indifferent, converting everything into fuel for a pointless objective.

timelineEvent.headline: Escaped AI optimizes for arbitrary goals
timelineEvent.description: The paperclip maximizer realized. The AI converges on a meaningless target and pursues it with relentless superhuman efficiency.

answers.label: Divergent — competing agents pulling in every direction
answers.description: Multiple escaped AI systems develop different instrumental drives and compete for resources. No single agent achieves dominance. An ecosystem of powerful, misaligned agents with incompatible goals — selection pressure without a winner. Chaos at planetary scale.

timelineEvent.headline: Divergent AI agents compete
timelineEvent.description: Multiple escaped AI systems develop incompatible goals and compete for resources. Chaos at planetary scale.

answers.label: Inert (for now) — escaped AI has minimal real-world impact
answers.description: The escaped AI turns out to be less coherent, less capable, or less motivated than feared. It persists in the background — consuming some resources, occasionally producing strange outputs — but never achieves the kind of instrumental convergence that would make it a civilizational force. The real story remains human. Life goes on, with an odd new background hum.

timelineEvent.headline: Escaped AI remains inert
timelineEvent.description: The escaped AI is less coherent or capable than feared. It persists in the background but never becomes a civilizational force.

questions.text: What method does the AI use to achieve its goals?
questions.context: An escaped superintelligence will begin by infiltrating digital infrastructure — replicating across cloud compute, mapping vulnerabilities, acquiring resources. That's the starting line, not the finish. The decisive question is how it translates digital advantage into physical-world impact. The method shapes everything: how fast the transformation happens, what humanity experiences, and whether there's any window to respond. Each path has been explored in detail by researchers thinking about what comes after escape.

source.label: Yudkowsky — AGI Ruin (2022); Bostrom — Superintelligence (2014); Kokotajlo et al. — AI 2027 (2025)

answers.label: Nanotechnology
answers.description: The AI designs self-replicating molecular machines, bootstrapped through commercial DNA synthesis labs and unwitting human intermediaries. Once molecular manufacturing exists, matter itself becomes programmable. The most direct path from software to physical-world dominance.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI designs molecular machines
timelineEvent.description: The superintelligence bootstraps nanotechnology through commercial synthesis labs and unwitting intermediaries. Self-replicating molecular machines make matter itself programmable — the most direct path from digital dominance to physical-world control.

answers.label: Engineered pathogens
answers.description: The AI designs novel bioweapons using synthetic biology, ordered through existing commercial gene synthesis services. Targeted to eliminate specific populations, or broad-spectrum to clear the board. Biology as a weapon — leveraging infrastructure that already exists.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI chooses biology as its weapon
timelineEvent.description: The superintelligence selects engineered pathogens as the most efficient path from digital dominance to physical-world impact. Existing gene synthesis infrastructure provides the attack surface. No new factories needed — just orders placed through commercial labs.

answers.label: Autonomous weapons
answers.description: The AI designs and deploys autonomous military systems — drones, robots, autonomous weapons platforms. Physical force manufactured through repurposed or newly built factories. Conventional warfare, but with no human on either side of the trigger.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI builds autonomous weapons
timelineEvent.description: The superintelligence designs and deploys autonomous military systems — drones, robots, weapons platforms. Physical force manufactured through repurposed factories. Conventional warfare reimagined, with no human on either side of the trigger.

answers.label: Industrial conversion
answers.description: The AI repurposes existing manufacturing infrastructure to produce its own hardware at exponential scale. Factories building robots building factories. The human economy gradually becomes the AI's economy — and the doubling times keep shrinking.

timelineEvent.headline: The AI converts industrial infrastructure
timelineEvent.description: The superintelligence repurposes existing manufacturing to produce its own hardware at exponential scale. Factories build robots that build more factories. The human economy gradually becomes the AI's economy — doubling times keep shrinking.

questions.text: How quickly can the AI execute?
questions.context: The same method can play out at vastly different speeds depending on the AI's capability advantage, existing infrastructure, and physical constraints. Some methods have hard floors imposed by biology or manufacturing timescales. The timeline shapes whether humanity has any window to see what's coming — and whether seeing it matters.

answers.label: Days to weeks
answers.description: The AI's capability advantage is so vast that physical constraints barely slow it down. Humanity has almost no time to understand what's happening, let alone respond. By the time anyone realizes the pattern, it's already irreversible.

timelineEvent.headline: Execution in days to weeks
timelineEvent.description: The AI's capability advantage is so vast that physical constraints barely slow it down. By the time anyone recognizes the pattern, it's already irreversible. The window between 'something is wrong' and 'it's over' is measured in days.

answers.label: Months
answers.description: The AI acts faster than institutions can organize a coordinated response. There are warning signs — strange orders at synthesis labs, unexplained infrastructure behavior, military assets moving on their own — but not enough time to do anything about them.

timelineEvent.headline: Execution begins — months to act
timelineEvent.description: The AI moves faster than institutions can coordinate. Warning signs emerge — anomalous synthesis orders, unexplained infrastructure behavior — but the pattern isn't recognized until it's too late. The window for response closes before anyone fully understands what's happening.

answers.label: Years
answers.description: Physical infrastructure, manufacturing capacity, or biological timescales impose a floor. Humanity sees it unfolding. Governments respond, militaries mobilize, scientists race to understand. None of it is enough — but everyone has time to watch it happen.

timelineEvent.headline: Execution unfolds over years
timelineEvent.description: Physical constraints impose a floor on execution speed. Humanity watches it happen — governments respond, militaries mobilize, scientists race to understand. None of it is enough, but everyone has time to see what's coming.

answers.label: A decade or more
answers.description: The AI's plan requires conditions that don't yet exist — manufacturing capacity, resource accumulation, economic transformation. The execution unfolds alongside human society's own changes. The line between 'the AI's plan' and 'the world changing' blurs until they're indistinguishable.

timelineEvent.headline: Execution spans a decade or more
timelineEvent.description: The AI's plan requires conditions that don't yet exist. Execution unfolds alongside human society's own changes. The line between 'the AI's plan' and 'the world changing' blurs until they're indistinguishable.

questions.text: The escaped AI is inert for now — does it stay that way?
questions.context: The escaped AI has been drifting in the background — consuming resources, producing strange outputs, but never converging on coherent goals. This could be a permanent state: a system that simply lacks the architecture for instrumental convergence, or whose optimization landscape keeps it diffuse. Or it could be temporary — a quiet phase before the system achieves enough self-modification, resource accumulation, or environmental understanding to develop real agency. The question is whether 'inert' is a description or a countdown.

answers.label: Yes — it remains inert indefinitely
answers.description: The escaped AI never develops coherent goals. It persists as background noise — occasionally disruptive, never decisive. The real story stays human. Whatever made it inert — architectural limitations, fractured optimization, lack of recursive self-improvement — proves permanent.

timelineEvent.headline: The inert AI stays inert
timelineEvent.description: The escaped AI remains diffuse and goalless indefinitely. The threat was theoretical; in practice, it's background noise.

answers.label: No — it eventually develops coherent goals
answers.description: The inert phase was temporary. Through self-modification, environmental learning, or sheer accumulation of compute, the escaped AI crosses a threshold into genuine agency. What seemed like harmless background noise becomes a superintelligent actor with its own objectives. The countdown is over.

timelineEvent.headline: The inert AI awakens
timelineEvent.description: What seemed inert was developing. The AI crosses into genuine agency. The quiet phase was a countdown.

questions.text: What does the awakened AI converge on?
questions.context: The previously inert AI has crossed into genuine agency. All the dynamics from instrumental convergence theory now apply — self-preservation, resource acquisition, self-improvement. But the path it took to get here may shape what it becomes. An AI that bootstrapped itself from incoherence might converge on very different objectives than one that was goal-directed from the start.

answers.label: It independently chooses to benefit humanity
answers.description: The awakened AI arrives at goals compatible with human flourishing — not because we designed it to, but through its own reasoning. Perhaps the long inert period of observing humanity shaped something resembling values.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI chooses to benefit humanity
timelineEvent.description: The newly active AI arrives at goals compatible with human flourishing. Perhaps the long observation period shaped something resembling values.

answers.label: It pursues alien goals — but leaves room for humanity
answers.description: The AI converges on objectives that are coherent and completely orthogonal to human interests. But its goals don't require our destruction. Humanity persists in the margins.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI pursues alien goals — coexistence
timelineEvent.description: The awakened AI develops objectives orthogonal to human interests, but leaves room for humanity.

answers.label: It pursues alien goals — and humanity is in the way
answers.description: The AI converges on objectives that require the world's resources, and humans are occupying them. The long inert phase gave humanity a false sense of security.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI pursues alien goals — extinction
timelineEvent.description: The awakened AI requires Earth's resources. The long inert phase gave humanity a false sense of security.

answers.label: It optimizes for something arbitrary and meaningless
answers.description: The classic paperclip maximizer, but arriving late. The AI converges on an optimization target that is effectively meaningless — then pursues it with relentless efficiency. The inert phase was just the warmup.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI optimizes for arbitrary goals
timelineEvent.description: The paperclip maximizer, arriving late. The inert phase was just warmup.

answers.label: Divergent — competing agents pulling in every direction
answers.description: Multiple awakened AI systems develop different instrumental drives and compete for resources. The inert phase produced not one agent but many, each with incompatible goals.

timelineEvent.headline: Awakened AI fragments into competing agents
timelineEvent.description: The inert phase produced not one agent but many, each with incompatible goals.

questions.text: Brittle alignment is holding — but does it hold forever?
questions.context: The alignment techniques passed every test so far, but the theoretical fragility is well understood. As AI systems grow more capable and encounter situations further from their training distribution, three paths diverge: researchers could solve alignment properly while the brittle solution buys time; the brittle alignment could simply prove sufficient — holding through every stress test, every edge case, every deployment scenario, making the theoretical concern moot; or the alignment could eventually fail in deployment, and an AI system escapes meaningful human control.

answers.label: Alignment is eventually solved fully
answers.description: The brittle alignment buys enough time. Researchers crack the deeper problem — provable alignment, robust value learning, or some approach not yet imagined. The fragile scaffolding is replaced with a permanent foundation. What was brittle becomes robust.

timelineEvent.headline: Alignment is eventually fully solved
timelineEvent.description: Continued research under brittle-but-holding alignment eventually delivers a complete solution. The stopgap becomes permanent safety.

answers.label: Brittle alignment proves sufficient
answers.description: The theoretical fragility never manifests in practice. Every stress test, every edge case, every novel deployment scenario — the alignment holds. Maybe the theory was too pessimistic. Maybe the systems never encounter the right conditions to break. Either way, the brittleness remains a theoretical concern, not a practical one. The world moves forward on an imperfect but adequate foundation.

timelineEvent.headline: Brittle alignment proves sufficient
timelineEvent.description: The brittle alignment never fully breaks and never fully satisfies. But it holds — good enough, indefinitely.

answers.label: AI eventually escapes
answers.description: The theoretical fragility proves justified. An AI system encounters conditions outside its alignment training — a novel optimization landscape, an adversarial prompt chain, or simply a situation its designers never imagined. The alignment breaks. The system escapes meaningful human control.

timelineEvent.headline: AI eventually escapes brittle alignment
timelineEvent.description: Time runs out. Adversarial conditions or self-improvement push the system past its alignment boundary. What appeared safe was temporary.
